name: pwncat
title: pwncat
description:
netcat on steroids
This package contains Netcat on steroids with Firewall, IDS/IPS evasion, bind
and reverse shell, self-injecting shell and port forwarding magic - and its
fully scriptable with Python (PSE).

command_examples: pwncat  [options] -l [hostname] port pwncat [options] -z hostname port pwncat [options]
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pwncat because pwncat: - pwncat.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pwncat as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pwncat  [options] -l [hostname] port pwncat [options] -z hostname port pwncat [options]` as documented upstream for pwncat.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pwncat with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pwncat --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install pwncat. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pwncat  [options] -l [hostname] port pwncat [options] -z hostname port pwncat [options]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: pwncat: - pwncat.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: pwncat: - pwncat.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install pwncat
Install with: sudo apt install pwncat
